serviceNotes.engine oil: Mfr row: later W211 diesel model years often use ASSYST flexible plans with engine oil and filter up to 25,000 km or 12 months per EU-oriented maintenance summaries (oil-change.info style W211 diesel data); earlier OM646 cars may use 15,000 km fixed plans—follow the in-dash indicator and printed booklet. Rec row: for short-trip or severe use, 15,000 km oil changes are a conservative specialist recommendation when longer flexible intervals are shown.
serviceNotes.dust filter: Mercedes-Benz specifies dust filter (cabin filter) replacement on time and distance criteria in the European maintenance programme (typically every 24 months and/or with major inspections).
serviceNotes.engine air filter: Air filter replacement follows Mercedes inspection intervals (commonly 60,000 km or 48 months on EU sheets for this generation).
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Brake fluid must be renewed every 24 months per Mercedes-Benz; interval is time-based only.
serviceNotes.check brake pad thickness: Brake pads are inspected at each service; 30,000 km provides a mid-cycle reminder for high-mileage driving between oil services.
serviceNotes.fuel filter: Diesel fuel filter replacement is specified in the manufacturer schedule (typically 60,000 km for OM646/OM642 E-Class diesels; verify exact engine variant in the printed booklet).
serviceNotes.coolant: Long-life coolant exchange is specified at high mileage in Mercedes maintenance data for W211; 150,000 km is a commonly quoted renewal point—verify for your market booklet.
serviceNotes.automatic transmission fluid: 722.6/722.9 transmissions are often lifetime-fill officially; preventive ATF service near 60,000 km is widely recommended by independent Mercedes specialists.
serviceNotes.manual transmission oil: Manual gearbox oil is typically long-life; changing near 100,000 km is a common workshop practice.
serviceNotes.transfer case oil: 4MATIC models only. When Mercedes lists no routine interval, many specialists renew transfer case fluid around 60,000 km.
serviceNotes.front axle differential oil: 4MATIC models only. Coordinate with transfer case service for AWD drivetrain longevity.
serviceNotes.rear axle differential oil: Differential oil is often lifetime officially; 60,000 km renewal is a preventive recommendation, especially on 4MATIC vehicles.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: If OEM interval is deferred, a full exchange around 120,000 km restores additive protection.
serviceNotes.poly-V belt: Accessory drive belt replacement near 100,000 km avoids sudden failure of charging and ancillaries.
